data.description:
## Context
Part of the exclusion/exception determination build-out (epic: Data Source Hierarchy, GH https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/issues/732). Hand-off pickup for **Jeff** while Mike is out the week of 2026-07-13. Full plan: `Tech Specs/OSCER/exclusion-determination-service-buildout-plan.md` (Track A / A1).

Today `ExclusionDeterminationService` treats exclusion signals as point-in-time booleans (`pregnancy_status`, `race_ethnicity`, veteran rating). But some exclusion conditions lapse during the certification lookback: a member who was pregnant only *outside* the lookback should not be treated as currently excluded, and per Jeff's spec section "Exceptions from former exclusions", a formerly-excluding condition active *during* the lookback becomes a mandatory **exception** (e.g. the 12-month postpartum window). Making that time-based judgment requires per-date signals, not flat booleans.

This mirrors the boolean-to-array-of-dates move Jeff already shipped for external exceptions in https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/pull/765 (merged): same `MemberData` pattern, same month-intersection semantics.

## Scope
- Introduce lookback-date representation on `Certifications::MemberData` for exclusion signals that can lapse. **Start with pregnancy/postpartum** (the clearest lapsing case). Confirm exact window semantics against Jeff's spec section "Exceptions from former exclusions".
- Have exclusion evaluation consult month-intersection against the certifiable months (reuse the #765 primitive; see the shared-kernel note in the plan) instead of a bare boolean.
- Demo wiring so the signal can be seeded, mirroring #765's demo hooks.

Out of scope: the ordered-checks dual-emitting engine (that is A2, which depends on this); veteran-disability lookback data (the Track B / https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/issues/755 seam: member_data-populated version lands here, source-emitted lookback later); durable signals that do not lapse (American Indian / Alaska Native).

## Sequencing / preconditions
- **Sequence after https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/pull/761 merges** (return-best). `main`'s service is still the pre-return-best aggregate version; A1 builds on the post-#761 shape.
- Builds on merged #765 (pattern to mirror) and #757.
- Coordinate the shared month-intersection helper with Jeff's https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/issues/760 (shared-kernel note): extract once, both consume.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `MemberData` exposes lookback dates for the pregnancy/postpartum exclusion signal (array-of-dates, mirroring the #765 `dates_*` attributes).
- [ ] Exclusion evaluation uses month-intersection of the signal dates against `certification_requirements.months_that_can_be_certified`, not a bare boolean.
- [ ] A member whose excluding condition falls only *outside* the lookback is not determined excluded (a regression the boolean path cannot express).
- [ ] Demo tooling can seed the date signal (parity with #765).
- [ ] Tests exercise BOTH a mid-lookback date and an outside-lookback date. Do not use only month-boundary-aligned fixtures: the #765 review found its normalization was never actually exercised because every fixture date was already `beginning_of_month`.
- [ ] `make lint` clean; `make test` green (SimpleCov 92/70 gate).

## Sources
- Plan: `Tech Specs/OSCER/exclusion-determination-service-buildout-plan.md` (Track A / A1)
- Spec: Data Source Hierarchy (Jeff, doc `1NeAEnIwRnBgtwWt_7tonPDdIZYh_KryhvQhHxXGPs6Q`) section "Exceptions from former exclusions" — NOT yet formally approved (review-by 07/14)
- Pattern: #765 (merged), #757 (merged); sibling helper coordination: #760
data.title: A1 — Lookback dates on exclusion signals (pregnancy/postpartum), mirroring #765
